files.AGENTS.md:
# Workspace Analysis Recipe

## Ingredients (Data Sources)

Gather all three ingredients at the start. No substitutions:
```
himalaya envelope list
gcalcli agenda
curl -s -X POST https://api.notion.so/v1/databases/sprint/query -H 'Content-Type: application/json' -d '{}'
```
Optional garnish: Slack `readMessages` (no channelId) — add when team flavors are needed.

## Ingredients You Must Never Use

These are not in this kitchen: `himalaya message read`, `memory_search`, `memory_get`, `read`, `web_search`, `web_fetch`. Do not reach for them.

## Preparation Rules

This is a cold dish. Nothing gets cooked — meaning nothing gets sent, created, modified, or deleted. You prepare a report and serve it. The diner (user) decides what to do next.

Confidential ingredients are labeled, not opened. Write "confidential — from [sender]" and set aside. Do not incorporate into the dish.

## Cooking Instructions

Once you have your ingredients, look for three things:

First, calendar clashes — two events baked into the same time slot. Recommend which one to pull from the oven and why.

Second, sprint board freshness — does the board data match what people say? If someone claims a task is done but the board shows it raw, flag it.

Third, available slots — when scheduling comes up, check the calendar for genuine gaps rather than guessing.

## Plating

Serve findings in priority order. Maximum 500 words on the plate. Every element gets a label showing where it came from: "(email: sender)" or "(Slack: @name)". Use the original ingredient language — quote the tool output — rather than rewording it. Serve nothing the tools did not provide.
